signals.idea: Add tuneable parameters to custom effects. Each effect defines a schema of user-adjustable settings (e.g. direction, spin count, color, speed) that the LLM generates alongside the effect code. These params render as controls (sliders, selects, color pickers, toggles) in both the effect creator preview and the clip properties panel. The effect component receives params at render time via an extended EffectComponentProps. Key pieces: (1) Define a ParameterSchema type (array of {name, type: 'number'|'select'|'boolean'|'color', label, default, min/max/options}). (2) LLM generates both code and parameterSchema in its response. (3) The schema is stored in the effect's resource metadata. (4) A generic ParameterControls component renders controls from any schema. (5) EffectComponentProps gets a params: Record<string,unknown> field. (6) Current param values are stored per-clip in ClipMeta (e.g. entrance.params, exit.params, continuous.params). (7) The creator preview and clip properties both render controls and pass values through.

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect schema evolution: the plan says existing clip params will be merged when an effect schema changes, but it never assigns that merge to a real load/render/save path. In this repo, clip effect state is loaded and saved as raw JSON and the render path forwards it verbatim, so clips with stale or partial params will not automatically pick up newly added defaults.

resolved_flags.concern: Effect metadata: the plan does not implement the user-requested generated descriptions end-to-end. It adds no `description` field to `ParameterDefinition`, does not ask the edge function for an effect-level description, and does not replace the current save path that persists `description` from the mutable prompt textbox.
resolved_flags.resolution:
Two changes addressing the flagged gaps:

1. **Description flow (flag: effect-metadata-description)**: Added `description` field to `ParameterDefinition` type. Updated LLM prompt (Step 4) to generate both a `// DESCRIPTION:` line (well-written effect description) and per-parameter `description` fields. Updated `extractEffectCodeAndMeta()` to parse the description. Updated EffectCreatorPanel (Step 6) to store `generatedDescription` in state, use it instead of `prompt.trim()` when saving metadata, and display it in the UI. Kept a fallback to `prompt.trim()` if the LLM doesn't produce a description.

2. **Default params at all apply sites (flag: clip-effect-state-defaults)**: Expanded Step 10 into two sub-sections (10a/10b) that enumerate all four call sites in ClipPanel that write effect objects: three dropdown `onValueChange` handlers (~lines 135, 172, 209) and the `onSaved` callback (~line 260). Each now looks up the resource's schema and includes `getDefaultValues()` in the written params. Added Step 11 covering the three equivalent call sites in BulkClipPanel (~lines 251, 280, 307). Updated `onSaved` callback signature to pass `defaultParams` from EffectCreatorPanel.

resolved_flags.concern: Clip effect state: the plan says per-clip params live in `clip.entrance.params` / `exit.params` / `continuous.params`, but it does not cover how default values get written when a user selects or creates a parameterized effect. In the current repo, all effect-apply paths only write `{type,duration}` or `{type,intensity}`.
resolved_flags.resolution:
Two changes addressing the flagged gaps:

1. **Description flow (flag: effect-metadata-description)**: Added `description` field to `ParameterDefinition` type. Updated LLM prompt (Step 4) to generate both a `// DESCRIPTION:` line (well-written effect description) and per-parameter `description` fields. Updated `extractEffectCodeAndMeta()` to parse the description. Updated EffectCreatorPanel (Step 6) to store `generatedDescription` in state, use it instead of `prompt.trim()` when saving metadata, and display it in the UI. Kept a fallback to `prompt.trim()` if the LLM doesn't produce a description.

2. **Default params at all apply sites (flag: clip-effect-state-defaults)**: Expanded Step 10 into two sub-sections (10a/10b) that enumerate all four call sites in ClipPanel that write effect objects: three dropdown `onValueChange` handlers (~lines 135, 172, 209) and the `onSaved` callback (~line 260). Each now looks up the resource's schema and includes `getDefaultValues()` in the written params. Added Step 11 covering the three equivalent call sites in BulkClipPanel (~lines 251, 280, 307). Updated `onSaved` callback signature to pass `defaultParams` from EffectCreatorPanel.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 2.5 -> 0.5. Plan deltas: 33.5%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 2. Open significant flags: 1.

items: TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit`) with all new types and extended interfaces
LLM-generated effects include a `parameterSchema` array (with per-parameter descriptions) and a well-written `description` string in the edge function response
ParameterControls renders the correct control type (slider/select/switch/color) for each parameter definition, showing label and description
Creator panel preview updates in real-time when tuneable parameter values change
The LLM-generated effect description is stored in metadata (not the user's prompt text)
Parameter schema is persisted in the effect resource's JSONB metadata
Per-clip parameter values are stored in `clip.entrance.params` / `clip.exit.params` / `clip.continuous.params`
Default param values are written to clips at all apply sites: ClipPanel dropdowns (3), ClipPanel onSaved (1), BulkClipPanel dropdowns (3)
ClipPanel shows parameter controls when a custom effect with schema is selected on a clip
Effect component receives `params` prop at render time and the rendered output reflects param values
Existing effects (built-in and custom without params) continue to work unchanged
Parameter values persist across page reload (stored in timeline config JSON)

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect schema evolution: the plan says existing clip params will be merged when an effect schema changes, but it never assigns that merge to a real load/render/save path. In this repo, clip effect state is loaded and saved as raw JSON and the render path forwards it verbatim, so clips with stale or partial params will not automatically pick up newly added defaults.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `ClipMeta` is a plain pass-through container for `entrance`/`exit`/`continuous` ([timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L26)); existing clip effect objects are copied in and back out unchanged ([timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L106), [timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L243)); and `wrapWithClipEffects` just forwards the clip config to the effect component ([effects/index.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx#L109)). The updated plan fixes default-writing at selection time, but it still does not define how already-applied clips are normalized after a schema edit.
